nodes.title: Clients Overview
nodes.summary: codewiki keeps user attention on hidden knowledge while Pi surfaces a four-tab status control room with Product/System/Clients grouping, kanban roadmap progress, named execution ownership, and resume-first workflow through a TUI-first model.

nodes.title: Roadmap State and TUI
nodes.summary: Derived roadmap and status read models plus first-party Pi panel rules for a kanban-style roadmap surface with blockers, evidence, agent ownership, and resume visibility inside codewiki.

nodes.title: Status Surfaces v4
nodes.summary: `/wiki-status` and `Alt+W` open one live panel organized as Wiki | Roadmap | Agents | Channels, with a repo-plus-traffic-light header, an optional one-line footer summary, and separate `/wiki-config` configuration.

nodes.title: Product
nodes.summary: codewiki gives Pi a repo-local, docs-first navigation and execution contract for development projects.

nodes.title: Extension Runtime
nodes.summary: Extension owns bootstrap, four-tab status rendering, resume execution, and internal roadmap and session operations for repo-local wikis discovered from current cwd or targeted explicitly by repo path or picker.

nodes.title: System Overview
nodes.summary: codewiki is organized around package surface, extension runtime, starter templates, and generated metadata.

nodes.title: Package Surface
nodes.summary: codewiki ships one extension and one skill as a globally installable Pi package surface with a small public workflow centered on bootstrap, status, resume, and separate configuration.

nodes.title: System Rules
nodes.summary: System-level documentation contract for maintaining codewiki itself with hidden knowledge, machine evidence, roadmap state, and status read models.

nodes.title: Templates and Rebuild
nodes.summary: Starter templates and rebuild script define canonical on-disk contract for bootstrapped repos.

nodes.title: Roadmap
nodes.summary: Numbered, trackable delta tasks for codewiki.

nodes.title: Karpathy LLM Wiki pattern
nodes.summary: Persistent compiled wiki sits between raw sources and query-time reasoning; index and chronological log matter because knowledge should compound instead of being re-derived every query.

nodes.title: lucasastorian/llmwiki implementation
nodes.summary: Production implementation shows useful navigation ideas: path-based wiki tree, overview hub, log chronology, search/read/write MCP tools, citation rendering, and batch reads for broad navigation across many docs.

nodes.title: Finish simplified research/specs/roadmap refactor
nodes.summary: Replace legacy top-level taxonomy with simplified contract across templates, runtime prompts, README, and package docs.

nodes.title: Generate brownfield spec hierarchy from repo structure
nodes.summary: Setup/bootstrap now infer first-pass boundary specs from meaningful repo structure so brownfield repos start closer to real ownership seams.

nodes.title: Emit roadmap-ready tasks from drift audits
nodes.summary: Self-drift and code-drift flows should output roadmap task objects so unresolved deltas can be promoted with minimal manual rewriting.

nodes.title: Decide compact history strategy after archive deprecation
nodes.summary: Need explicit answer for how historical docs changes are preserved once archive is no longer default top-level bucket.

nodes.title: Support roadmap update and close mutations
nodes.summary: After append flow, package should let agents update or close existing roadmap tasks without manual roadmap JSON editing.

nodes.title: Link Pi sessions to roadmap tasks
nodes.summary: Keep Pi session JSONL native, append custom task-link entries, and read active task focus directly from Pi session state instead of maintaining repo-owned session caches.

nodes.title: Expand smoke coverage for full public command surface
nodes.summary: Smoke tests should fail if any documented public command disappears from the packaged extension.

nodes.title: Support global install with cwd-based wiki discovery
nodes.summary: Extension runtime should load from a global Pi package install while discovering the active repo-local wiki from current cwd.

nodes.title: Prototype roadmap browsing interactions for Pi
nodes.summary: Extension delivered roadmap-browsing groundwork that validated task inspection flows before the public UX was later consolidated into fewer commands.

nodes.title: Consolidate public UX into four intelligent wiki commands
nodes.summary: Refactor the public command surface down to /wiki-bootstrap, /wiki-status, /wiki-fix, and /wiki-review while keeping roadmap/task/session internals and granular tools behind the scenes.

nodes.title: Add roadmap-state read model and first-party roadmap widget
nodes.summary: Generate a read-only roadmap-state UI model and render a compact first-party roadmap widget so users can track focused, in-progress, and next tasks inside Pi without expanding the public command surface or persisting session caches.

nodes.title: Rename package surface from codebase-wiki to codewiki
nodes.summary: Rename package, extension, skill, tool, repo, and runtime identifiers from codebase-wiki/codebase_wiki to codewiki.

nodes.title: Review codewiki skill and global AGENTS alignment
nodes.summary: Review codewiki skill guidance and global AGENTS overlay against the Karpathy-style baseline and align naming around codewiki.

nodes.title: Support self-dogfood migration to wiki and .wiki
nodes.summary: Let codewiki discover and operate a self-hosted repo using wiki/.wiki so this repo can dogfood the agreed structure.

nodes.title: Add wiki-code implementation resume command
nodes.summary: Expose a user-facing wiki-code command that resumes the focused roadmap task or picks the next open task and queues implementation work.

nodes.title: Add explicit repo targeting and fallback project picker for wiki commands
nodes.summary: Let global wiki commands accept an explicit repo path, offer a project picker when cwd has no wiki, and explain global-vs-local targeting clearly in command errors.

nodes.title: Add explicit repo targeting to internal codewiki tools
nodes.summary: Let internal codewiki tools accept explicit repo paths so global installs work cleanly for tool-driven mutations outside repo cwd.

nodes.title: Design and implement status dock v1
nodes.summary: Replace the flat wiki-status experience with a status-state read model and a unified persistent dock that highlights spec drift, roadmap coverage, and next action at a glance.

nodes.title: Keep status dock visible across new global sessions and replace wiki-status follow-up with roadmap next task
nodes.summary: Keep the status dock visible across global/new-session fallback and make /wiki-status surface the next roadmap task directly in deterministic output.

nodes.title: Refactor status panel around Wiki, Roadmap, Agents, and Channels
nodes.summary: Refactor the status panel into a minimal control room with circle-only health, a repo-only header, wiki grouped as Product/System/Clients, a kanban-style roadmap by phase gate, named agents, a minimal channels list, and footer-only ambient summary while keeping authored `wiki/ux` on disk for now.

nodes.title: Migrate codewiki to a .wiki-only knowledge contract
nodes.summary: Complete the .wiki-only migration closure by removing remaining registry/backlinks compatibility artifacts, cleaning stale generated outputs, and verifying the live repo runs cleanly on the graph-first derived contract.

nodes.title: Make goal quality first-class across foundational docs and task metadata
nodes.summary: Made goal quality first-class across foundational docs, starter templates, roadmap task metadata, generated views, and smoke coverage.

nodes.title: Add multi-cadence heartbeat drift analysis across product, system, UX, and code
nodes.summary: Refined heartbeat lanes so freshness is modeled as work-first: each lane now advertises causal work triggers, fallback max-age protection, and work-based stale reasons surfaced in generated status state and runtime status views.

nodes.title: Turn status surfaces into a resume-first startup control room
nodes.summary: Turned status surfaces into a resume-first startup control room by adding a deterministic generated resume view to status-state and making runtime summary, dock, panel, and text output prioritize focused work, verification cues, and heartbeat context over passive health-only reporting.

nodes.title: Add proposal-to-roadmap promotion and causality-preserving dedupe
nodes.summary: Add proposal-to-roadmap promotion and causality-preserving dedupe so same-user parallel Pi sessions can work in one repo without duplicate tasks, visible collisions, or any user-facing need to manage internal roadmap mutation tools.

nodes.title: Build a task todo-research-implement-verify-done loop with evidence gates
nodes.summary: Strengthen task execution so roadmap tasks move through one parent-owned `todo -> research -> implement -> verify -> done` progression with structured evidence, low-friction phase drivers, and blocked state treated as a card signal rather than a separate board column.

nodes.title: Collapse public command surface around /wiki-bootstrap, /wiki-status, /wiki-resume, and /wiki-config
nodes.summary: Remove deprecated public commands so codewiki's visible workflow converges on bootstrap, status, resume, and config with Alt+W as the status shortcut.

nodes.title: Add git-backed revision correlation and freshness tracking across wiki state
nodes.summary: Deepen codewiki integration with git by anchoring heartbeat, evidence, status, and task state to commits plus authored-spec digests so drift and resume logic can detect stale intent safely.

nodes.title: Add agent execution visibility to status read model and panel
nodes.summary: Expose who is doing what and when by deriving named agent execution rows with task ownership, mode, status, last action, and constraints into the status panel and roadmap cards.

nodes.title: Define pluggable channel routing for agent communications
nodes.summary: Define a pluggable channels contract while keeping the first `Channels` tab minimal: add-channel affordance plus the list of already added channels.

nodes.title: Rename UX pillar to Clients in authored wiki taxonomy
nodes.summary: Track the deferred authored taxonomy rename so status surfaces can say Clients now while the on-disk authored pillar remains `wiki/ux` until a later migration.
